task: D1.3 remaining same-input c=1 projection fusions
summary: Static decode dataflow and M.4 profile enumeration found no remaining standalone same-input c=1 projection fusion with an arithmetic/data-reuse win. Linear-attention QKV/Z, full-attention Q/K, linear-attention A/B, selected-MoE gate/up, and shared-expert gate/up are already fused. The only material unfused same-input slice is full-attention V beside the retained Q/K dual path (10 calls/token), but the parent full-attention Q/K/V pack8 widening prototype was correct and graph-safe yet slower/no-win, so D1.3 keeps defaults unchanged and leaves any narrower K/V-specific retest to D1.6.

candidate_inventory.name: linear_attn.in_proj_qkv + linear_attn.in_proj_z
candidate_inventory.pack8_layout: preserved; concatenated QKV/Z scratch then sliced

candidate_inventory.name: full_attn.q_proj + full_attn.k_proj
candidate_inventory.current_runtime: project_full_attention_qkv_fp16 -> gemv_awq_dual_pack8_transposed_fp16 for tokens == 1; v_proj stays single
candidate_inventory.pack8_layout: preserved; Q/gate and K are contiguous in q_proj_key scratch

candidate_inventory.name: linear_attn.in_proj_a + linear_attn.in_proj_b
candidate_inventory.layout: dense dual output scratch split into A/B

candidate_inventory.name: selected_moe.gate_proj + selected_moe.up_proj
candidate_inventory.pack8_layout: preserved selected-dual pack8 output consumed by fused SiLU/down input

candidate_inventory.name: shared_expert.gate_proj + shared_expert.up_proj
candidate_inventory.current_runtime: shared_expert_paro_w4_fp16 uses dual pack8 for packed sidecars; shared_expert_w8a16_fp16 uses precombined gate_up W8A16 path
candidate_inventory.pack8_layout: packed PARO W4 sidecars preserve dual-pack8 layout; legacy W8A16 is not a D1.3 pack8 target

candidate_inventory.name: full_attn.v_proj adjacent to retained q_proj+k_proj
candidate_inventory.current_status: only_material_unfused_same_input_slice; rejected for D1.3 broad widening
candidate_inventory.current_runtime: project_full_attention_qkv_fp16 calls v_proj through project_pack8_fp16 after Q/K dual
candidate_inventory.estimated_bucket: part of w4_single_gemv 50 calls/token; V accounts for 10 of those 50 calls/token, while O and linear-attn out are not same-input neighbors
candidate_inventory.why_not_implemented: A D1.3 implementation would widen the retained Q/K dual pack8 family to Q/K/V (or change scratch layout for a separate K/V dual). Parent already tested full-attention Q/K/V pack8 widening and reverted it because the larger kernel shape erased launch-count savings; this adds no arithmetic/data-reuse win under graph replay.

candidate_inventory.name: full_attn.o_proj, linear_attn.out_proj, selected/shared down, lm_head
candidate_inventory.reason: These are single projections whose inputs are attention/GDN/MoE/final-norm outputs, not adjacent projections over the same input row. Fusing them would be producer/post-op work covered by D1.2, D1.4, D5.1, or D5.2 rather than D1.3 same-input projection fusion.

full_attention_qkv_pack8.recheck: 2026-05-11 current graph stack only noise-level movement: 512/128 115.569 vs 115.258; 4K/128 120.622 vs 120.655.
full_attention_qkv_pack8.prefill_wiring: diagnostic same-command prefill regressed: ~905 tok/s control -> 836 Q/K-only -> 822 Q/K/V.
full_attention_qkv_pack8.lesson: Do not widen a fused projection family unless it preserves kernel efficiency or adds real data reuse; graph replay makes pure launch-count wins very small.

rationale: M.4 shows generic W4 single pack8 remains visible (11.59-13.56% of decode kernel time), but static inventory attributes only 10/50 calls/token to an unfused same-input V projection; the rest are output projections/downstream singles.
All other adjacent same-input c=1 projection pairs are already fused in hipEngine while preserving pack8/repacked layouts.
The only broader remaining pack8 widening is parent-rejected full-attention Q/K/V; it is launch-count-only and changed kernel shape more than it saved dispatches.
No arithmetic/data-reuse-only D1.3 win was identified; do not add another diagnostic kernel unless D1.6 explicitly reopens the K/V-specific hypothesis with a stronger design.
decision.follow_up: D1.6 remains the narrower k_proj+v_proj row if a future task wants to test a targeted K/V design; it should inherit the parent Q/K/V rejection as the baseline caution.

validation.result: passed; HIP runtime loads, py_compile passed, pytest layout+decode-state suite passed (69 tests), artifact JSON parsed
